task: I need to make a quick plan for a drive to a couple of places in the area and I don’t want to waste time zigzagging around. What’s the easiest way to line up the stops so the route stays simple?

turns.agent_response:
The easiest way is to line them up in a straight-ish path so you’re not doubling back.

Quick rule:
1. Pick your starting point.
2. Put the stops in geographic order from one side of town to the other.
3. Do the farthest stop first only if it keeps you moving in one direction.
4. Avoid crossing back over the same area.

If you want, send me the actual two places and I’ll tell you the simplest order right away.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s the basic idea. But I’d want it a little more practical than that.

If you’ve got 2 or 3 stops, line them up by:
- closest to farthest from where you start
- or west to east / south to north, whichever keeps you from cutting back
- put the place with the parking hassle first if it’s going to slow you down

I usually check the map for the one-way streets and the turnarounds too. That’s where the time gets wasted, not just distance.

If you want the clean route, give me the actual stops and where you’re leaving from. Otherwise it’s just general advice.

turns.agent_response:
A simple rule:

- Start with the stop that’s **on the way out** from where you’re leaving.
- Put the other stops in **one direction only**: north to south, east to west, or closest to farthest.
- If one place has **hard parking or a slow entrance**, do that one first.
- Don’t add a stop if it means **turning back across town** or re-crossing the same area.

Quick test:
- **Same road / same side of town**: fold it in.
- **Different direction or forces a U-turn**: skip it for another day.

If you want, send the stops and your starting point, and I’ll line them up in the simplest order.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s closer. Still a little too neat, though.

What I’d actually do:
- start with the one that’s closest to my first turn out of the house
- then stack the rest in the same general direction
- if one place has annoying parking or a slow lot, hit that first so it’s done
- if a stop pulls me back across the same streets, I’d leave it for another day unless it’s important

And I’d check the map for one-way streets before I leave. That’s usually where the stupid time gets burned.

If you want, give me the actual places and where you’re starting from, and I’ll put them in order without the extra talk.

turns.agent_response:
That’s the right way to do it.

Simple version:
- start with the stop closest to your first turn out
- keep the rest going the same direction
- do the annoying parking stop first
- skip anything that makes you backtrack across the same streets unless it matters

And yes, check one-way streets before you go. That’s usually where the wasted time hides.

If you send the places and your starting point, I can put them in order.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah. That’s fine. Simple enough and not dressed up.

That’s basically how I’d do it anyway. First turn out, same direction, annoying parking first, and don’t go chasing your own tail across town for no good reason.

The one-way streets part matters. People always act like distance is the whole thing, and it isn’t.
